hypothesis: Augmenting SMILES/SELFIES chemical language models with an equivariance-inducing gradient regularizer (equigrad) during pretraining will increase their CKNNA alignment with 3D atomistic MLIPs on QM9 by at least 0.1 absolute and reduce their zero-shot or fine-tuned QM9 energy-prediction MAE by at least 10% relative to identical language models trained without equigrad.
